content.text:
<user_query>
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
import requests
import os

GOOGLE_MAPS_URL = "https://share.google/VBUDEGd8VBNXWNVEC"

# WEEKLY BATCH NAME
REVIEW_BATCH = "2026_week_21"

DOWNLOAD_FOLDER = f"weekly_reviews/{REVIEW_BATCH}"

os.makedirs(DOWNLOAD_FOLDER, exist_ok=True)

with sync_playwright() as p:

    browser = p.chromium.launch(
        headless=False
    )

    page = browser.new_page()

    print("")
    print("Opening Google Reviews...")
    print("")

    page.goto(GOOGLE_MAPS_URL)

    page.wait_for_timeout(8000)

    print("")
    print("MANUAL STEPS:")
    print("1. Open Reviews/Ulasan")
    print("2. Sort by Terbaru")
    print("3. Scroll recent reviews")
    print("4. Click ONE food photo")
    print("5. Wait until full-size viewer opens")
    print("")

    input("Press ENTER after full-size photo is visible...")

    images = page.locator("img").all()

    downloaded = 0
    seen_urls = set()

    for i, img in enumerate(images):

        try:

            src = img.get_attribute("src")

            if (
                src
                and "googleusercontent" in src
                and "=s" in src
            ):

                # Remove thumbnail sizing
                clean_url = src.split("=")[0]

                # Skip duplicates
                if clean_url in seen_urls:
                    continue

                seen_urls.add(clean_url)

                print("")
                print(f"Downloading image {downloaded}")

                response = requests.get(clean_url)

                filename = f"food_{downloaded}.jpg"

                filepath = os.path.join(
                    DOWNLOAD_FOLDER,
                    filename
                )

                with open(filepath, "wb") as f:
                    f.write(response.content)

                downloaded += 1

        except Exception as e:
            print("ERROR:", e)

    print("")
    print("===================================")
    print(f"Downloaded {downloaded} images")
    print(f"Saved to: {DOWNLOAD_FOLDER}")
    print("===================================")

    input("Press ENTER to close...")
</user_query>

You shared a Playwright script for downloading Google Maps review food photos — I'll check the workspace and any prior context to see what you need.

[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

You pasted a **weekly Google Maps review photo downloader** — it fits a ramen shop workflow (collect customer food photos for your site or social). Playwright is already on your machine; here is how it works, what to watch for, and a tighter version. ## What your script does 1. Opens your Google Maps share link in Chromium (visible window). 2. Waits for you to open Reviews → sort **Terbaru** → scroll → open **one** food photo in the full-size viewer. 3. After you press Enter, scans every `` on the page. 4. Keeps URLs with `googleusercontent` and `=s` (Google’s size parameter). 5. Strips sizing with `split("=")[0]` and saves unique images to `weekly_reviews/2026_week_21/`. ## How to run it (VS Code) ```powershell cd C:\Users\FUNNY\Desktop\ramen-shop # or wherever you save the .py file pip install playwright requests playwright install chromium python download_reviews.py ``` Change `REVIEW_BATCH` each week (e.g. `2026_week_22`). ## Likely issues | Issue | Why | |--------|-----| | **0 downloads** | `requests.get` without browser headers often gets **403** from Google | | **Wrong images** | `page.locator("img").all()` grabs avatars, map tiles, tiny thumbs — not only the lightbox | | **Broken URLs** | `split("=")[0]` can truncate URLs that use `=` elsewhere | | **Duplicates / tiny files** | Same photo at many sizes; small `=s40` thumbs slip through | | **Browser left open** | No `browser.close()` until you press Enter again | ## Improved approach (main fixes) - Scope images to the **photo viewer** (or largest images only). - Clean URLs with a regex: remove `=s123` / `=w123-h456` instead of splitting on `=`. - Reuse the **browser session** (cookies + Referer) when downloading. - Skip images below a minimum width when possible. ```python import os import re import requests from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright GOOGLE_MAPS_URL = "https://share.google/VBUDEGd8VBNXWNVEC" REVIEW_BATCH = "2026_week_21" DOWNLOAD_FOLDER = f"weekly_reviews/{REVIEW_BATCH}" MIN_WIDTH = 200 # skip tiny thumbs os.makedirs(DOWNLOAD_FOLDER, exist_ok=True) def clean_google_image_url(src: str) -> str: # Remove size/crop params like =s0, =w800-h600, =s0-d return re.sub(r"=s\d+.*$|=w\d+-h\d+.*$", "", src) with sync_playwright() as p: browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=False) context = browser.new_context() page = context.new_page() print("\nOpening Google Maps...\n") page.goto(GOOGLE_MAPS_URL, wait_until="domcontentloaded") page.wait_for_timeout(8000) print("MANUAL STEPS:") print("1. Open Reviews / Ulasan") print("2. Sort by Terbaru") print("3. Scroll recent reviews") print("4. Click ONE food photo") print("5. Wait for full-size viewer\n") input("Press ENTER when full-size photo is visible...") # Prefer viewer/lightbox; fall back to all large images imgs = page.locator('[role="dialog"] img, [data-photo-index] img, img').all() session = requests.Session() for cookie in context.cookies(): session.cookies.set(cookie["name"], cookie["value"]) downloaded = 0 seen_urls = set() for img in imgs: try: src = img.get_attribute("src") if not src or "googleusercontent" not in src: continue box = img.bounding_box() if box and box["width"] < MIN_WIDTH: continue clean_url = clean_google_image_url(src) if clean_url in seen_urls: continue seen_urls.add(clean_url) resp = session.get( clean_url, headers={"Referer": "https://www.google.com/"}, timeout=30, ) resp.raise_for_status() filepath = os.path.join(DOWNLOAD_FOLDER, f"food_{downloaded}.jpg") with open(filepath, "wb") as f: f.write(resp.content) print(f"Saved food_{downloaded}.jpg") downloaded += 1 except Exception as e: print("ERROR:", e) print(f"\nDownloaded {downloaded} images → {DOWNLOAD_FOLDER}\n") input("Press ENTER to close...") browser.close() ``` ## Tie-in to your ramen site After a batch downloads: 1. Pick the best shots from `weekly_reviews/2026_week_21/`. 2. Copy them to `ramen-shop/images/` (e.g. `gallery-1.jpg`). 3. Point your CSS/HTML at those files instead of Unsplash. --- What do you want next? - **A** — Save this as a project file and walk through first run step-by-step - **B** — Debug a specific error (0 downloads, 403, wrong images) - **C** — Wire downloaded photos into your ramen site gallery automatically Reply with **A**, **B**, or **C** (and paste any error text if **B**). [REDACTED]

0 downloads usually means the filter never matched or downloads failed silently. I'll check your setup and pinpoint which case applies.

Identifying the cause: the `=s` filter is too strict — Google's full-size viewer often uses `=w`/`=h` or no size suffix, so every image was skipped with no errors. Updating the scraper to match those URLs and add brief debug output.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string: input("Press ENTER after full-size photo is visible...") images = page.locator("img").all() session = requests.Session() for cookie in context.cookies(): session.cookies.set(cookie["name"], cookie["value"]) downloaded = 0 seen_urls = set() stats = { "total_imgs": len(images), "google_urls": 0, "skipped_old_equals_s_rule": 0, "skipped_small": 0, } print("") print(f"Scanning {stats['total_imgs']} elements on page...") print("") for img in images: try: box = img.bounding_box() if box and box["width"] < 120: stats["skipped_small"] += 1 continue for src in collect_image_urls(img): if not src or "googleusercontent" not in src: continue stats["google_urls"] += 1 # Old script required "=s" — full-size viewer often uses "=w" or no suffix if "=s" not in src: stats["skipped_old_equals_s_rule"] += 1 clean_url = clean_google_image_url(src) if clean_url in seen_urls: continue seen_urls.add(clean_url) print(f"Downloading image {downloaded}...") response = session.get( clean_url, headers={"Referer": "https://www.google.com/"}, timeout=30, ) response.raise_for_status() filename = f"food_{downloaded}.jpg" filepath = os.path.join(DOWNLOAD_FOLDER, filename) with open(filepath, "wb") as f: f.write(response.content) downloaded += 1 except Exception as e: print("ERROR:", e) print("") print("--- Debug ---") print(f"Total tags: {stats['total_imgs']}") print(f"googleusercontent URLs seen: {stats['google_urls']}") print( "URLs that old script skipped (no '=s'): " f"{stats['skipped_old_equals_s_rule']}" ) print(f"Skipped (width < 120px): {stats['skipped_small']}") print("-------------")

content.text: Here’s what went wrong and what to do next. ## Why you got **0 downloads** Your script only downloaded when **all three** were true: ```python src and "googleusercontent" in src and "=s" in src ``` That third check is the problem. In the **full-size photo viewer**, Google often serves images like: - `...=w1920-h1080` (width/height, **not** `=s`) - `...=s0` or no size suffix at all - real URL in **`srcset`**, while `src` is a tiny placeholder So the loop ran over every ``, found nothing matching `=s`, and finished with **0 downloads** and **no ERROR lines** — it looked like it “worked” but saved nothing. That’s a silent filter miss, not a Playwright or folder bug. ## Other things that can still block downloads | Issue | Symptom | |--------|---------| | Photo viewer not open when you press Enter | Few or no `googleusercontent` URLs | | `requests` without browser cookies | `ERROR: 403` after the fix | | ENTER pressed too early | Same as above | ## What I changed I updated `C:\Users\FUNNY\restaurant_review_audit\monthly_review_scraper.py` to: 1. **Drop the `=s` requirement** — accept any `googleusercontent` URL 2. Read **`srcset`** and **`data-src`** (not only `src`) 3. Use **browser cookies + Referer** so Google doesn’t return 403 4. Print **debug stats** so you can see what was found vs skipped ## Run it again ```powershell cd C:\Users\FUNNY\restaurant_review_audit python monthly_review_scraper.py ``` After you press Enter, you should see something like: ``` Scanning 47 elements on page... Downloading image 0... --- Debug --- Total tags: 47 googleusercontent URLs seen: 12 URLs that old script skipped (no '=s'): 12 <-- this explains your 0 before ``` If `googleusercontent URLs seen: 0`, the viewer wasn’t open yet — repeat the manual steps and press Enter only when the big photo is on screen. If you see URLs found but still `Downloaded 0` with `ERROR: 403`, paste that output and we can tune the next step. [REDACTED]

A **Chromium window** opens → follow the prompts: - Open **Reviews / Ulasan** - Sort **Terbaru** - Scroll recent reviews - Click **one food photo** until the **full-size** viewer is open 4. In the **terminal**, press **Enter**. 5. You should see lines like `Downloading image 0...` and a debug block. 6. Press **Enter** again to close the browser. ## Where files go `C:\Users\FUNNY\restaurant_review_audit\weekly_reviews\2026_week_21\` ## What to look for in the output **Good (fix worked):** ``` Scanning 40 elements on page... Downloading image 0... Downloaded 3 images ``` **Still stuck — viewer not ready:** ``` googleusercontent URLs seen: 0 Downloaded 0 images ``` → Open the big photo viewer, then press Enter again (re-run the script). **Auth blocked:** ``` ERROR: 403 Client Error ``` → Paste the full terminal output here and we’ll adjust. --- I can’t press Enter or use the browser for you from Cursor’s sandbox. 0 downloads with the viewer open usually means URLs never matched or downloads failed silently. Adding network capture (images load via XHR, not always ``) and stronger diagnostics. [REDACTED]
Google Maps often loads photos via network requests and shadow DOM, not plain `` — updating the scraper to capture those. [REDACTED]

**0 downloads** with the improved script usually means the script never saw any photo URLs — not that saving failed. On Google Maps, review photos often **don’t show up as normal `` tags** in the full-size viewer. They load through **background images**, **shadow DOM**, or **network requests** — so your old scan found nothing to download. Your output also didn’t show the `--- Debug ---` block, which suggests either an older copy of the script ran or the output was summarized. ## What I changed (v3) The scraper at `C:\Users\FUNNY\restaurant_review_audit\monthly_review_scraper.py` now: 1. **Records network responses** while you browse (from page load through Enter) — catches photos as the browser actually fetches them 2. **Scans the DOM deeply** (including shadow DOM + `background-image`) 3. **Downloads with Playwright’s request API** (same cookies as the browser — fewer 403s) 4. **Prints clear debug counts** so we can see *why* it’s 0 ## Run again ```powershell cd C:\Users\FUNNY\restaurant_review_audit python monthly_review_scraper.py ``` Do the manual steps, then press **Enter only when the big black photo viewer is open** (not just the reviews list). ## How to read the new output | Debug line | Meaning | |------------|---------| | `Network photo responses captured: 0` | Browser never loaded a big photo — viewer wasn’t open, or wrong step | | `Network ... 5+` but `Downloaded 0` | URLs found but download blocked — paste full output | | `Downloaded 3 images` | Working | **Good example:** ``` Network photo responses captured: 4 DOM photo URLs found: 2 Unique URLs to try downloading: 4 Downloading image 0... Downloaded 3 images ``` **Still broken:** ``` Network photo responses captured: 0 DOM photo URLs found: 0 No photo URLs found. ``` → Full-size viewer wasn’t open; try again and wait 2–3 seconds on the big photo before Enter. --- After you run it, paste the full **`--- Debug ---`** section here (and any `ERROR:` lines). That tells us the next fix in one step. [REDACTED]
